I found several recipes on RecipeTin Eats that match your search! Here are the top results:

1. **One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole** - A made-from-scratch creamy, cheesy Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole made in one pot that tastes like risotto but is much more straightforward to make. The recipe involves melting butter and heating oil in a pot, adding onion and garlic for 1 minute, then cooking chicken until it changes from pink to white.

2. **Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry** - A big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli with lots and lots of sauce, because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce but the cook still wants some to soak the rice.

3. **One Pot Chinese Chicken and Rice** - A Chinese Chicken and Rice made all in one pot that tastes like fried rice and Chinese marinated chicken, plus plenty of greens to make a complete meal.

4. **Emergency Chicken Rice Casserole** - A dump-and-bake Chicken Rice Casserole with broccoli made from scratch with creamy, cheesy, perfectly cooked rice and juicy chicken.

All of these recipes feature chicken, broccoli, rice, and garlic in various combinations. You can visit RecipeTin Eats directly to access the full recipes with detailed instructions and ingredient lists!


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the meal name, description, full list of ingredients, step-by-step recipe overview, prep time, cook time, and the URL of the main recipe image.

tool_invocations.result:
# One Pot Chicken Broccoli Rice Casserole

**Meal Name:** One Pot Broccoli, Chicken and Rice Casserole

**Description:** "Made from scratch, this tastes like a risotto but is so much more straight forward to make! A complete meal in one pot."

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes
**Cook Time:** 20 minutes

**Ingredients:**
- 2 tbsp unsalted butter
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 500g chicken thigh, bite-sized pieces
- 2.5 tbsp flour
- 2 cups milk
- 2 cups chicken broth
- 1¼ cups long grain white rice, uncooked
- 1 tsp dried thyme
- Salt and pepper
- 1 large head broccoli, cut into small florets
- 2 cups shredded mozzarella or cheddar cheese
- Finely chopped parsley (optional)

**Recipe Steps Overview:**
1. Cook onion, garlic, and chicken until chicken turns white
2. Add flour and stir for 1 minute
3. Gradually add milk, then broth
4. Add rice, thyme, salt, and pepper; simmer covered for 12 minutes
5. Add broccoli and cook 3 minutes more
6. Stir in half the cheese and broil with remaining cheese until melted
7. Serve with optional parsley garnish

**Recipe Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2018/01/One-Pot-Chicken-Broccoli-Rice_7.jpg

tool_invocations.name: WebFetch

args.prompt: Get the meal name, description, full list of ingredients, step-by-step recipe overview, prep time, cook time, and the URL of the main recipe image.

tool_invocations.result:
# Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry Recipe

**Meal Name:** Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry (extra saucy!)

**Description:** "Big batch stir fry made with chicken and broccoli, and lots and lots of sauce - because broccoli florets hold lots of sauce but I still want some to soak my rice!"

**Prep Time:** 10 minutes
**Cook Time:** 6 minutes

**Main Recipe Image URL:** https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2020/07/Chicken-Broccoli-Stir-Fry_2.jpg

**Key Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken breast, thinly sliced
- 1½ tsp baking soda
- 2 tbsp cornflour
- 3 tbsp light soy sauce
- 1½ tbsp Chinese cooking wine
- 3 tbsp oyster sauce
- 2 tsp sesame oil
- 400g broccoli florets
- ½ onion, sliced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1¼ cups water
- White rice

**Recipe Steps Overview:**
1. Tenderize chicken breast
2. Steam or boil broccoli until cooked
3. Prepare sauce by mixing cornflour and remaining ingredients
4. Cook aromatics (garlic and onion) in hot oil
5. Add and cook chicken until done
6. Combine broccoli, sauce, and water; simmer 1-2 minutes until glossy
7. Serve over rice

metric_results.reasoning: The response strongly prioritises expiry: the critical ingredients are chicken breast and broccoli at 1 day, rice is also urgent at 2 days, and garlic is near-term at 4 days. Both meals include the most urgent items in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, and they are placed first and second in the 2-meal array, so ordering is appropriate. Coverage is high because all expiring inputs appear in usesIngredients across the meals, but expiringIngredients only lists chicken breast and broccoli, omitting rice and garlic there, which is a minor gap preventing full alignment.
All 2 meals have required fields and response is valid JSON

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 2 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.
